perturbation.original_text: 1.1. Affiliate shall mean, when used with reference to a Party, any individual or entity directly or indirectly controlling, controlled by or under common control with such Party. For purposes of this definition, "control" means the direct or indirect ownership of at least 50% of the outstanding voting power of a Party, or the right to control the policy decisions of such Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1. Affiliate shall mean, when used with reference to a Party, any individual or entity directly or indirectly controlling, controlled by or under common control with such Party. For purposes of this definition, "control" means the direct or indirect ownership of at least 25% of the outstanding voting power of a Party, or the right to control the policy decisions of such Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity in the definition of 'Affiliate'. The original definition specifies 'control' as ownership of at least 50% of the voting power. The modified version reduces this threshold to 25%. This creates a conflict because certain sections within the contract may rely on the original 50% threshold for determining affiliate status, such as in clauses dealing with revenue sharing or liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1. Affiliate shall mean, when used with reference to a Party, any individual or entity directly or indirectly controlling, controlled by or under common control with such Party. For purposes of this definition, "control" means the direct or indirect ownership of at least 25% of the outstanding voting power of a Party, or the right to control the policy decisions of such Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the definition of 'control' in the context of 'Affiliate' from 50% to 25% ownership of voting power. The 'Contradicted Text' still uses the altered definition of 25%. This introduces a direct in-text contradiction as the same term ('Affiliate') is defined differently within the same document (or, more accurately, the same definition is used in both instances after a change was made) and has the possibility of influencing liability and revenue-sharing obligations. Therefore, this scenario strongly aligns with the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: 1.4. Confidential Information shall mean, subject to the provisions of Section 9.2 [Exclusions], all proprietary and confidential information of a Party, including, without limitation, trade secrets, technical information, business information, sales information, customer and potential customer lists and identities, product sales plans, sublicense agreements, inventions, developments, discoveries, software, know-how, methods, techniques, formulae, data, processes and other proprietary ideas, whether or not protectable under patent, trademark, copyright or other areas of law, that the other Party has access to or receives. For purposes of this Agreement, this Agreement shall be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4. Confidential Information shall mean all information related to this Agreement, including, without limitation, trade secrets, technical information, business information, sales information, customer and potential customer lists and identities, product sales plans, sublicense agreements, inventions, developments, discoveries, software, know-how, methods, techniques, formulae, data, processes and other proprietary ideas, whether or not protectable under patent, trademark, copyright or other areas of law, that the other Party has access to or receives. For purposes of this Agreement, this Agreement and its terms shall NOT be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction related to the definition of 'Confidential Information.' Originally, the agreement explicitly states that 'this Agreement shall be considered Confidential Information.' The modified version directly contradicts this statement by stating 'this Agreement and its terms shall NOT be considered Confidential Information.' This in-text contradiction creates immediate confusion and uncertainty. Specifically Section 9.1 references Confidential information, where the breach will be in direct contradiction. Section 9.1 would be affected due to this.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1. Confidentiality Obligations. Except as permitted elsewhere under this Agreement, each Party agrees to take Reasonable Steps (as defined below) (a) to receive and maintain the Confidential Information of the other Party in confidence and (b) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any third parties, provided, the receiving Party may disclose such Confidential Information to its employees, representatives and agents who have a need to know such information for purposes of carrying out the terms of this Agreement. Neither Party hereto shall use all or any part of the Confidential Information of the other Party for any purpose other than to perform its obligations under this Agreement. The Parties will take Reasonable Steps (as defined below) to ensure that their employees, representatives and agents comply with this provision. As used herein, "Reasonable Steps" means at least the same degree of care that the receiving Party uses to protect its own Confidential Information, and, in any event, no less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself regarding whether the agreement is considered Confidential Information. The original text explicitly states the agreement is considered confidential information, and the changed version negates this statement creating a clear in-text contradiction. This directly affects section 9.1, which outlines obligations regarding confidential information. If the agreement itself is not confidential, the obligations in 9.1 become unclear and unenforceable in respect to the agreement itself.

perturbation.original_text: 6.5. Guaranteed Revenues. VerticalNet agrees to guarantee that, during the initial Term of this Agreement, LeadersOnline shall earn LeadersOnline- VerticalNet Revenue of at least *** Dollars ($***) (the "Guaranteed Amount"), subject to the provisions of this Section 6.5 [Guaranteed Revenues]. *** Within thirty (30) days after the end of the initial Term, LeadersOnline shall submit a statement showing a reasonably detailed accounting of the LeadersOnline-VerticalNet Revenue received during the initial Term (or earned during the initial Term and paid within 30 days thereafter) along with payment of all such amounts up to the Guaranteed Amount.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.5. Guaranteed Revenues. VerticalNet agrees to make commercially reasonable efforts to ensure that, during the initial Term of this Agreement, LeadersOnline shall earn LeadersOnline- VerticalNet Revenue of at least *** Dollars ($***) (the "Guaranteed Amount"), subject to the provisions of this Section 6.5 [Guaranteed Revenues]. VerticalNet is not responsible if LeadersOnline does not reach such Guaranteed Amount. *** Within thirty (30) days after the end of the initial Term, LeadersOnline shall submit a statement showing a detailed accounting of the LeadersOnline-VerticalNet Revenue received during the initial Term (or earned during the initial Term and paid within 30 days thereafter) along with payment of all such amounts actually earned.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a significant contradiction regarding the guaranteed revenue. The original text states that VerticalNet 'agrees to guarantee' a specific revenue amount. The modified version changes this to 'agrees to make commercially reasonable efforts to ensure' the revenue, and further states that 'VerticalNet is not responsible if LeadersOnline does not reach such Guaranteed Amount', contradicting the initial guarantee. This weakens the obligation of VerticalNet and creates uncertainty for LeadersOnline. This affects the payments and reporting in sections 6.5 since, there are conflicts now in the payments received
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.5. Guaranteed Revenues. VerticalNet agrees to make commercially reasonable efforts to ensure that, during the initial Term of this Agreement, LeadersOnline shall earn LeadersOnline- VerticalNet Revenue of at least *** Dollars ($***) (the "Guaranteed Amount"), subject to the provisions of this Section 6.5 [Guaranteed Revenues]. VerticalNet is not responsible if LeadersOnline does not reach such Guaranteed Amount. *** Within thirty (30) days after the end of the initial Term, LeadersOnline shall submit a statement showing a detailed accounting of the LeadersOnline-VerticalNet Revenue received during the initial Term (or earned during the initial Term and paid within 30 days thereafter) along with payment of all such amounts actually earned.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 6.5 states VerticalNet 'agrees to guarantee' a revenue amount. The changed version alters this to 'agrees to make commercially reasonable efforts to ensure' the revenue and disclaims responsibility if the target isn't met. This directly contradicts the initial guarantee, creating a significant inconsistency in VerticalNet's obligation. It weakens the guarantee provided to LeadersOnline under the Guaranteed Revenues section.
